payload.value:
Per docs/commit-noise-design.md (act-6018), implement bundling so a typical 'work an issue end-to-end' loop produces ~1 git commit instead of ~4-5. Reduces 69% of current commits-since-v0.1.0 (the act-op bookkeeping noise) toward zero.

**Refined design (orchestrator call, 2026-05-10):** bundle inside the claim→close window only; preserve immediate-publish for ops that need cross-agent coordination.

- **claim** auto-commits + pushes immediately (preserves coordination — other agents see 'this is taken' before they pick the same issue).
- **close** auto-commits + pushes; bundles all the issue's pending act-op files (everything since the claim) into a single commit.
- **All other ops within a claim→close window** (update, dep add, redact, etc.) write the op file but DO NOT commit — they ride the close commit.
- **Ops outside a claim→close window** (filing a new issue from scratch, dep adds on issues you don't own) auto-commit as today.
- --no-commit preserved as today for explicit batching across multiple issues.

This is the sharper version of the original auto-bundle-on-close idea: only claim+close need coordination urgency; intra-session ops can bundle freely. Result: 1 commit per issue lifecycle in the common case (claim, work, close — close commit bundles everything except claim).

**Things the implementation must get right:**
- Detect 'in a claim window' state per-issue (e.g. claim op exists with no subsequent close from same node).
- Rollback semantics: if close fails mid-batch, the partial pending state unstages cleanly.
- Concurrency: two agents on different issues in the same tree must not stomp on each other's pending batches.
- act doctor: must still recognise (act-XXXX) markers in bundled close commits.
- act log <id>: timeline reconstructed from .act/ files (source of truth) is unchanged regardless of git-commit grouping.
- Migration: existing v0.1.0 commits stay; only future ops bundle. No history rewriting.
- Config knob: .act/config.json bundle_strategy = per_op | per_session (default) so existing repos can opt out.
